chunk_summary: This section focuses on CMAM program performance indicators and Sphere benchmarks for managing severe acute malnutrition (SAM), emphasizing discharge follow-up and program quality standards in humanitarian contexts.
learning_objectives: Understand the Sphere benchmarks for SAM management in community-based programs.
Identify key performance indicators for CMAM program evaluation.
Learn the importance of discharge follow-up in improving SAM recovery rates.
Explore global guidelines and frameworks for SAM management.
key_facts: Sphere benchmarks recommend a cure rate of >75%, a death rate of <10%, and a default rate of <15% for SAM programs.
Community-based management of acute malnutrition (CMAM) is endorsed by WHO, UNICEF, WFP, and UNSCN since 2007.
The NITI Aayog report highlights India’s efforts in scaling CMAM programs to address malnutrition in vulnerable populations.
The Sphere Handbook provides minimum standards for humanitarian response, including nutritional interventions.
WHO guidelines emphasize the prevention and management of wasting and nutritional edema in SAM cases.
reference_quote: Sphere benchmarks recommend a cure rate of >75%, a death rate of <10%, and a default rate of <15% for SAM programs. (Source: Sphere Handbook, 2011)
